items.title: Former Victorian premier John Brumby in dispute with neighbours over fence

items.title: Australian Border Force seize 20 million illegal vapes since January 2024 as crackdown continues

items.summary: The family of a young Indigenous woman who died in a NSW jail has told an inquest that she was a talented artist who generously supported anyone in need. The inquest is examining Corrective Services NSW's search and body scan practices.

items.title: 'Optimism' as Walk for Truth ends despite no formal commitment from PM
items.summary: The Walk for Truth ended at Parliament House today and while there was not a firm commitment from the PM, many felt the response was a 'positive move'.

items.title: Whyalla locals call for more certainty around steelworks' future amid news of final bidders
items.summary: Whyalla locals welcome more certainty about the future ownership of the city's steelworks, but say it is still not enough to warrant business investment and workers coming back to the Spencer Gulf.

items.title: Maroons let 20-0 lead slip in Origin I after Kalyn Ponga sent off late
items.summary: James Tedesco scores a spectacular try with 90 seconds left in State of Origin I, capping off a 20-point comeback after Queensland fullback Kalyn Ponga is sent off for a shoulder charge.

items.title: Labor MP who unseated Peter Dutton defends electoral commission referral over empty block
items.summary: Dickson MP Ali France has been referred to the Australian Electoral Commission with claims she is registered to vote at a vacant suburban block in her north Brisbane electorate.

items.title: Albany bakery cops fines over $60,000 after inspectors find rodent faeces and animal blood on bread
items.summary: Inspectors found animal blood dripping onto bread, rodent faeces on food and spreading mould at the Albany French Hot Bread Shop, triggering prosecution and $60,000 in penalties for the owners.

items.title: Maugean skates released back into Macquarie Harbour after two years in captivity
items.summary: A pair of endangered rays taken from a Tasmanian harbour as a species "insurance policy" have been released back into the wild after helping to produce more than 400 eggs for a captive research program.

items.title: ISIS-linked woman barred from Australia tried to board flight
items.summary: An Australian woman with links to ISIS, who is barred from Australia on a temporary exclusion order, tried to board a flight home with the other members of Tuesday's returning cohort, a Senate estimates hearing has been told.

items.title: No 'plan B' for submarines if AUKUS fails, says Defence Minister Richard Marles
items.summary: The defence minister brushes off calls for a "plan B" in case AUKUS fails, declaring it would be a step towards "giving up" on acquiring any new submarines at all.

items.title: Drought-affected farmers welcome significant rainfall but some miss out
items.summary: Up to 100 millimetres of rain has been welcomed across drought-hit parts of western NSW, but some farmers expecting relief were left disappointed.

items.title: Billions are traded each week on Kalshi and Polymarket. There is a push to bring prediction markets to Australia
items.summary: Kalshi and Polymarket have gone from obscure start-ups to two of the world's biggest prediction markets where users bet on yes-or-no questions spanning politics, pop culture and war.

items.title: Jim Chalmers flags 'legitimate' small business capital gains tax debate
items.summary: The government is treading carefully on carve-outs to its capital gains tax legislation amid backlash to its plan.

items.title: Pentagon denies reports of clash with SpaceX over Starlink price hike during Iran war
items.summary: The Pentagon has denied claims that the department has been clashing with Elon Musk's SpaceX over costs to use its satellite internet network, which the military's kamikaze drones rely upon in the war against Iran.

items.title: Measles cases rise in Australia as authorities monitor deadly outbreaks in South and South-East Asia
items.summary: Authorities keep a close eye on measles outbreaks in Asia, as many cases reported in Australia continue to be returned overseas travellers.

items.title: The government's proposed changes to the unemployment system, explained
items.summary: The federal government has proposed changes to Australia's $2 billion employment services system to make them “more responsive, effective and dignified”.

items.title: Adelaide track designer estimates MotoGP remodel could cost more than $100m
items.summary: Adelaide Street Circuit designer Bob Barnard estimates it could cost more than $100 million to remodel the track for the MotoGP, but the premier says that number has not been mentioned to him.

items.title: Cuts of meat that can save you money and how to cook with them
items.summary: If you are looking to trim your household food budget, lower-cost cuts of meat could be a good place to start. Here are some tips on what to look for and how to cook them.

items.title: Police search for swimmer missing at Koombana Bay in Bunbury
items.summary: Police say the woman entered the water at Koombana Bay just after 9:20am and has not been seen since

items.title: BOM plans to build new radar at Tennant Creek airport, 11 years after closure
items.summary: BOM says it has decided to install a new weather radar in Tennant Creek, in the same place as the previous radar.

items.title: Since placing his first bet at age 15, Dylan says he's lost at least $900k
items.summary: From footy tips to Taylor Swift bets, teenagers are being lured into gambling. Almost 34 per cent of Australian teens gamble and everyone from teachers, experts, to the young people themselves are sounding the alarm.

items.title: Concussion saw 15-year-old Macy Watson lose her 'spark', but a new trial helped her recover
items.summary: Macy became a shell of her usual self after suffering a concussion on a school trip, but experienced a huge improvement after a trial designed to help children.

items.title: Lord Howe Island invertebrates boom after rodent eradication
items.summary: Researchers say the removal of invasive rodents on Lord Howe Island has sparked a rebound of invertebrate life, including native cockroaches and other insects, vital to the broader ecosystem.

items.title: Scholarship to be created in memory of farmer Callum Smail after death at 26
items.summary: A scholarship will be created at a Victorian agricultural college in memory of a young man who died in a farming accident last month.

items.title: Alexia Putellas ends 14-season Barcelona career after fourth Champions League title
items.summary: Alexia Putellas ends her stint at Barcelona after winning 10 league titles and four Champions Leagues with the Spanish giants.

items.title: Australia's Kimberly Birrell stuns Jessica Pegula as Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka dazzle at French Open
items.summary: Australia's Kimberly Birrell continues a day of upsets for Australia by advancing to the second round of the French Open for the first time in her career.

items.title: Government pitches largest overhaul to unemployment system in decades
items.summary: Jobseeker services will shift away from a one-size-fits-all approach to a new tiered model, to be unveiled by the government later today.

items.title: St John Ambulance NT considering body-worn cameras amid ongoing assaults on paramedics
items.summary: The Northern Territory's ambulance service is considering introducing police-style body-worn cameras after the arrest of alleged murderer Jefferson Lewis led to rioting in Alice Springs last month.

items.title: NSW coach Laurie Daley says Nathan Cleary unfairly judged over losing State of Origin record

items.title: ASX jumps as odds of June rate rise move close to zero — as it happened
items.summary: Shares climbed on the ASX after softer than expected inflation figures sent market bets of a June rate rise to less than 10 per cent.

items.title: Rain brings potential 'opportunities' in renewed search for Gus Lamont, police say
items.summary: Eight months since Gus Lamont was reported missing, police have again returned to search Oak Park Station as part of an ongoing investigations into the four-year-old's disappearance.

items.title: Australian Adam Walton stuns former world number one Daniil Medvedev at French Open
items.summary: Queenslander Adam Walton makes it through the heat and five-set grind to down former world number one Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

items.title: Peer support worker Brandie McGrady is a Dubbo Drug Court success story

items.title: State of Origin star Cameron Munster's Maroons triumph anniversary brings heartache
items.summary: Cameron Munster's mum reveals the heartbreaking call she had to make before her superstar son led Queensland to victory days after the shock death of his dad.

items.title: Iran calls latest US strikes 'bad faith' sign as internet is slowly restored
items.summary: Iran has denounced US strikes in the country's south as a sign of "bad faith and unreliability" as negotiations toward a possible deal to end the war continue.

items.summary: Rare baby turtles that are a cross between the loggerhead and green species have been found on the beach at Mon Repos.

items.title: Wiradjuri community devastated by destruction of rock shelter by ACEREZ
items.summary: Members of a First Nations community in Central West NSW say they were devastated to learn a culturally significant rock shelter had been destroyed to make way for a renewable energy project.

items.title: Fidel Castro's daughter fled his regime. Now, she hopes the US can bring it down
items.summary: Fidel Castro's daughter says the US has failed to find a way to deal with Cuba for decades. But she tells the ABC that the Trump administration has a difference that could spell the end of the regime she fled.

items.title: Hungry eleven-armed seastars making a meal of Tasmanian scallops
items.summary: The fast spread of pest eleven-armed seastars has fishers, along with researchers, concerned.

items.title: Women and children linked to Islamic State spend first night on Australian soil
items.summary: A group of so-called ISIS brides who left the notorious al-Roj camp in Syria last week have spent their first night home in Australia.

items.title: BHP is rightly sensitive about its reputation as a corporate leader on climate change
items.summary: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.

items.title: Finding out you are adopted or donor-conceived after taking an ancestry DNA test
items.summary: Direct-to-consumer genetic testing kits are often given as gifts or purchased "as a bit of fun", which means people often aren't prepared to discover unexpected parents, siblings and other relatives.

items.title: Brad Scott sacking a chance for Essendon to step out of James Hird's shadow

items.title: Wallabies facing same selection headache as State of Origin's NSW Blues

items.summary: The fiercest rival in Australian rugby league resumes tonight, as New South Wales and Queensland meet in the opening match of the 2026 men's State of Origin series.

items.title: Iran war derailed talks to claw back billions in tax credits paid to miners

items.summary: If you think you know your stuff when it comes to State of Origin, here's your chance to prove it.

items.summary: The British capital's previous hottest May day was set only one day prior, when the mercury topped 33.5C.

items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: New documents reveal Bondi gunman Naveed Akram remained on ASIO and police radar in 2022
items.summary: Naveed Akram and his father travelled to Uzbekistan around 2022 and a former terror hotspot in the Philippines shortly before committing the Bondi massacre.

items.title: Ricky Hampson discovered he was a Stolen Generations survivor. Now, he's calling for action
items.summary: Ricky Hampson spent most of his life believing he had been adopted before learning several years ago he is a survivor of the Stolen Generations. Today, he is calling for more action and truth-telling.

items.title: Iluwanti Ken highlights mistreatment of APY Art Centre Collective after 'white hands on black art'
items.summary: Award-winning artist Iluwanti Ken says the withdrawal of funding and support over the "white hands on black art" scandal has left her feeling like a stranger in her own industry.

items.title: Police officer accused of rape sent message denying he was 'sleazy', court told
items.summary: A police officer goes on trial in South Australia's District Court after pleading not guilty to two counts of rape and one count of attempted rape.

items.title: Another group of women and children linked to Islamic State group return to Australia
items.summary: Two flights carrying women and children linked to the Islamic State group have landed in Australia.

items.title: Court orders developer Theo Onisforou not to contact Wingecarribee mayor, general manager
items.summary: A Southern Highlands property developer is only allowed to communicate with his local council's mayor and general manager through his lawyer under interim AVOs issued against him during a heated court appearance.

items.title: Shoko Kawata to become first mayor in Japan to take maternity leave while in office
items.summary: A Japanese mayor will take maternity leave this year, becoming the first in the country's history to do so while in office.

items.title: With 'advantage' shifting to Ukraine, Russia hits Kyiv with Oreshnik hypersonic missile
items.summary: Russia escalates attacks on Kyiv by using a hypersonic missile in one of its largest assaults on the Ukrainian capital since the start of the four-year war, and warns foreigners to leave the city ahead of further strikes.

items.title: BHP dumped plans for processing plant that would cut global emissions by 1.7 million tonnes a year
items.summary: Internal BHP documents reveal how the mining giant planned to set up an iron ore processing facility in the Pilbara to cut carbon emissions before backing away from it entirely.

items.title: K-pop stans score big and The Pussycat Dolls lead noughties nostalgia at 2026 American Music Awards
items.summary: Taylor Swift led the night with eight nominations but didn't take home a single award, while 80s rock star Billy Idol was honoured with a Lifetime Achievement Award.

items.title: Kumanjayi White family 'heartbroken' no charges laid over custody death
items.summary: The family of Kumanjayi White tell the ABC they are "heartbroken" that no charges will be laid over his death in custody, as Northern Territory Police confirm their criminal investigation has concluded.

items.title: Brinkworth family loses appeal over judgement that father was responsible for Blackford bushfire
items.summary: A court judgement that found pastoralist Tom Brinkworth liable for a 2021 bushfire, five months after he died, has been upheld after an appeal by Mr Brinkworth's family.
